title: InterruptPayload
description: Wire format for ctx.interrupt(payload) — the canonical HITL primitive. See spec/v1/interrupt.md for semantics. Discriminated by `kind`. The persisted `interrupt.requested` event carries this payload verbatim plus engine-assigned identity fields.

kind.description: Discriminator for resume-time routing + UI rendering + observability. Multi-Agent Shift Phase 4 adds `conversation.start` / `conversation.exchange` / `conversation.close` for multi-turn user interjections (see ConversationStartData / ConversationExchangeData / ConversationCloseData below). Phase 1 adds `low-confidence` for the confidence-escalation contract (orchestrator-supervisor suspends below threshold; see LowConfidenceData).

key.description: Deterministic key used to short-circuit on re-entry after process death. If two interrupt() calls in the same run emit the same key, the second returns the cached result of the first. Recommended: ${runId}:${nodeId}:${interruptCount}.

resumeSchema.description: Optional JSON Schema for resume value. Servers SHOULD validate before returning to the suspended executor.

timeoutMs.description: Optional auto-reject after this duration. Engine throws InterruptTimeoutError when exceeded.

ApprovalData.description: Payload for kind='approval'. Uses the canonical 5-action OpenWOP approval vocabulary.

actions.description: Allowed actions. Server MUST enforce on resolve. The 'ask' action does NOT exit the suspend — Q&A exchanges accumulate via askService until accept/reject/refine/edit-accept fires. See `interrupt.md` §`ApprovalResume` for the action vocabulary + per-action required fields.

approversList.description: Restrict resolution to these user IDs. Empty/unset = any user with approvals:respond scope.

ClarificationData.description: Payload for kind='clarification'. AI/system asks the user for missing context before continuing.

schema.description: Optional JSON Schema for the answer.

ExternalEventData.description: Payload for kind='external-event'. Engine waits for an external system to POST to /v1/interrupts/{token}.

eventType.description: Stable description of what's awaited. Surfaced in admin panels. Examples: 'stripe.checkout.completed', 'calendar.event.confirmed'.

correlation.description: Opaque correlation payload — whatever the external system needs to match the interrupt back to its source event.

CustomData.description: Escape hatch for kinds not yet spec'd. Servers MUST accept and persist; UI rendering and admin tooling are best-effort.

ConversationStartData.description: Payload for kind='conversation.start' (Multi-Agent Shift Phase 4). Opens a new multi-turn conversation context. The host mints a `conversationId` and emits `conversation.opened`; subsequent `conversation.exchange` calls reuse the same id.

conversationId.description: Opaque conversation identifier. Tenant-unique invariant: hosts MUST ensure no two distinct conversations in the same tenant share an id. Cross-host portability is not normative — `conversationId`s minted by one host MUST NOT be assumed resolvable by another.

title.description: Optional human-readable conversation title surfaced in host UIs.

initialPrompt.description: Optional system / agent message that opens the conversation. Folds into the `message` reducer as the first turn.

ConversationExchangeData.description: Payload for kind='conversation.exchange' (Multi-Agent Shift Phase 4). Single-turn suspend within an open conversation; resume value MUST validate against the per-turn `outcomeSchema`.

conversationId.description: Active conversation identifier — MUST equal the id minted by a preceding `conversation.start`.

outcomeSchema.description: Optional JSON Schema for the resume value (the user's response). Hosts SHOULD validate before resuming the suspended executor — replay determinism + idempotency require validated outcomes per the Phase-4 H4 disposition.

turnIndex.description: Optional 0-indexed turn within this conversation. Strictly monotonic when populated; absent values are derived host-side from event-log fold.

ConversationCloseData.description: Payload for kind='conversation.close' (Multi-Agent Shift Phase 4). Terminates an open conversation context. Final event in the conversation lifecycle; no further `conversation.exchanged` events MAY follow for this `conversationId` in this run.

LowConfidenceData.description: Payload for kind='low-confidence' (Multi-Agent Shift Phase 1 — confidence-escalation contract). Emitted by an agent (typically `core.orchestrator.supervisor`) when its `agent.decided.confidence` falls below the run's resolved escalation threshold. Run transitions to `'waiting-approval'`; an operator ratifies the decision via the resume path.

agentId.description: AgentRef.agentId of the agent that suspended.

threshold.description: Resolved escalation threshold for this run (default 0.7 per Phase-1 disposition; per-run override via `RunOptions.configurable.escalationThreshold`).

observed.description: The agent's reported confidence value that triggered the escalation.

decision.description: Optional sketch of the decision the agent WOULD have emitted at threshold-or-above. Lets operators see the un-ratified choice. Shape mirrors `OrchestratorDecision` or the agent's domain-specific decision type.
